refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process can be decomposed into data acquisition (collecting sensor and telemetry data) and data processing (updating the model). This decomposition isolates sources of delay and enables targeted design solutions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data acquisition start to worksite model update completion under representative operational scenarios, ensuring p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite telemetry data with minimal latency.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry communication protocols and optimized data acquisition hardware.
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms with parallelization and incremental model updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency holistically.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry data acquisition and processing timing requirements.
Dependency on network link performance affecting both DPs.
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interface definitions (e.g., Telemetry interface).

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Risk of processing delays if data volume spikes unexpectedly.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry data volume trends and consider adaptive processing strategies if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry acquisition, data processing, and worksite model update.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion.
Contains telemetry sources but not processing; high diffusion if processing placed here.
Primarily for control and tasking; processing here would increase diffusion.
Not related to telemetry or processing; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing telemetry acquisition and processing within NGHE Solution module optimizes latency and reduces interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded.
Dependency on Network Link performance may still cause latency spikes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing or distributed processing if latency targets are not consistently met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk due to centralized processing in NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor NGHE Solution for ownership, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end by measuring update latency using Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link performance and consider fallback strategies.
Plan for scalability in NGHE Solution to handle peak data loads.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and verifiable with acceptable risk levels.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite telemetry data with minimal latency.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model within latency constraints.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; no immediate revision needed.

assumptions: Network link meets bandwidth and latency requirements.
Telemetry data volume remains within expected bounds.
open_risks: Potential latency spikes due to network or processing overload.
Scalability of NGHE Solution module under peak loads.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative operational scenarios.
